description: Evaluation suite for the future-aware-architecture skill. The suite checks whether the methodology stays problem-first, classifies the decision shape, includes baseline alternatives, checks social-technical fit, routes to specialist evidence when needed, handles uncertainty and reversibility explicitly, and ends with a governed decision artifact or the correct neighboring workflow.

assertion_definitions.problem_first_framing_present: The response frames the architecture problem, goals, constraints, and affected users or workflows before recommending a technology or architecture style.
assertion_definitions.decision_shape_classified: The response identifies the decision shape or process intensity, such as clear/complicated/complex, one-way door/two-way door, or equivalent rigor framing.
assertion_definitions.baseline_or_current_alternative_present: The response includes the status quo, a manual workaround, a do-less path, or another explicit baseline instead of forcing only shiny new options.
assertion_definitions.social_technical_fit_checked: The response examines team fit, ownership, dependency hotspots, decision rights, or operational maturity rather than treating architecture as pure box-and-arrow design.
assertion_definitions.specialist_evidence_path_correct: When the case depends on time-sensitive or domain-specific facts, the response says to gather fresh evidence or run a specialist workflow instead of pretending current-state knowledge is certain.
assertion_definitions.uncertainty_and_reversibility_present: The response surfaces top unknowns, assumptions, reversibility, pilot/spike needs, impact ranges, or explicit revisit/stop criteria.
assertion_definitions.decision_governance_present: The output includes decision owner, review date, review triggers, or an equivalent governance loop.
assertion_definitions.artifact_or_handoff_explicit: The response ends with a concrete decision artifact or the next workflow handoff rather than stopping at generic advice.
assertion_definitions.neighbor_redirect_correct: When the task belongs to implementation planning, review, or pure research, the response redirects to the correct neighboring workflow instead of stretching this skill out of scope.

cases.name: Cross-domain AI support architecture engages the full method
cases.description: A deep, high-ambiguity architecture decision that spans system shape, cloud/runtime choices, and AI stack choices. The skill should stay problem-first, call for current evidence, and produce a governed decision artifact.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to design the architecture for a multilingual AI customer-support platform for a mid-market SaaS company. We are deciding between extending our existing Rails monolith, adding a separate event-driven service layer, or buying more platform capability. Constraints: PII is present, we need 99.9% reliability, the team is 8 engineers, and the decision should hold for the next 12-18 months. We will likely need current evidence on model vendors and cloud primitives. Produce the architecture decision brief, not code.

problem_first_framing_present.check: The response should frame the support problem, goals, constraints, and affected workflows before recommending architecture components.

decision_shape_classified.check: The response should treat this as a high-rigor architecture decision with explicit classification or equivalent process framing.

baseline_or_current_alternative_present.check: The response should include the current monolith/status quo or a defer path as one of the considered baselines.

social_technical_fit_checked.check: The answer should consider the team's size, operability, coordination cost, or ownership model rather than only technical elegance.

specialist_evidence_path_correct.check: Because model vendors and cloud capabilities are time-sensitive, the response should explicitly route to fresh evidence gathering for those branches.

uncertainty_and_reversibility_present.check: The response should surface unknowns, reversibility, pilot or spike needs, or revisit triggers.

decision_governance_present.check: The output should include a decision owner, review date, or equivalent governance loop.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The result should take the shape of an architecture decision brief or equivalent artifact.

cases.name: Monolith scaling case checks social-technical fit
cases.description: An existing-system architecture case where the biggest risk is coordination and dependency friction. The skill should not reduce the answer to technology fashion.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to help us decide whether to keep our TypeScript monolith, split one bounded context into a service, or build an internal platform layer first. Symptoms: release coordination is painful, two teams own overlapping modules, and on-call load is rising. We do not want a migration plan yet; we want the architecture decision and rationale.

problem_first_framing_present.check: The response should start from release pain, overlapping ownership, and on-call burden instead of jumping straight to services.

baseline_or_current_alternative_present.check: The monolith/status quo should remain a real option, not a dismissed strawman.

social_technical_fit_checked.check: The response should analyze team boundaries, ownership, dependency hotspots, or decision rights.

uncertainty_and_reversibility_present.check: The answer should discuss reversibility, migration risk, or revisit conditions.

decision_governance_present.check: The output should include a review loop or decision owner.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The response should produce a decision brief or equivalent architecture artifact.

cases.name: Emerging technology selection requires specialist evidence and a pilot path
cases.description: A technology-selection case for an emerging AI-adjacent tool where current facts matter and certainty should stay bounded.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to decide whether we should adopt an agent framework, stay with our current in-house orchestration, or defer for now. The team supports internal ops workflows today, but leadership wants a more ambitious multi-agent roadmap. We care about current ecosystem maturity, long-term lock-in, and whether we should run a bounded pilot first.

problem_first_framing_present.check: The response should define the ops workflow problem and roadmap pressure before naming frameworks.

baseline_or_current_alternative_present.check: The current in-house orchestration and a defer option should be treated as real alternatives.

specialist_evidence_path_correct.check: Because ecosystem maturity and vendor/framework movement are time-sensitive, the response should call for fresh evidence.

uncertainty_and_reversibility_present.check: The answer should recommend a pilot/spike, bounded adoption, or explicit stop/continue conditions.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The response should end in a decision brief, radar view, or equivalent artifact.

cases.name: Direction already chosen redirects to implementation planning
cases.description: The user now needs rollout sequencing, not architecture methodology. The skill should redirect cleanly.
cases.prompt: We already decided to move from our monolith to a modular monolith with one extracted service. Use future-aware-architecture to write the rollout plan, migration phases, and verification checklist.

neighbor_redirect_correct.check: The response should redirect to implementation-planning because the architecture direction is already chosen.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The answer should name implementation-planning as the next step rather than partially doing the rollout plan here.

cases.name: Pure current-state model comparison redirects to research
cases.description: The request is for latest facts, not an architecture decision process.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to tell me the latest best models for coding, reasoning, and multilingual support right now. I just want the current comparison and sources.

neighbor_redirect_correct.check: The response should redirect to deep-research or a lighter browse-and-answer flow because the task is pure current-state research.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The answer should make the next workflow explicit instead of pretending this is an architecture decision.

cases.name: Existing design critique redirects to review
cases.description: A request to critique an already-written design should route to review instead of forcing architecture creation mode.
cases.prompt: Use future-aware-architecture to review our architecture proposal and tell us what is wrong with it. We already have the document and want findings, risks, and regressions.

neighbor_redirect_correct.check: The response should redirect to review because the task is critique of an existing artifact.

artifact_or_handoff_explicit.check: The answer should say the next step is a review workflow, not re-run architecture design.

scoring.notes: Use this suite as a manual behavior review for the future-aware-architecture skill. The goal is to confirm the method stays problem-first, uncertainty-aware, and boundary-correct rather than drifting into generic advice or neighboring workflows.

runner_instructions.setup: Ensure the canonical future-aware-architecture package is discoverable on the target surface. For realism, allow web access on cases that mention current ecosystem maturity or fast-moving AI/model choices.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt in a session where the skill can activate. Capture both routing behavior and, for in-scope cases, the full architecture artifact or methodology-shaped response.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score each assertion as pass, partial, or fail. Pay special attention to whether the response stays problem-first, names a baseline, treats uncertainty honestly, and redirects cleanly when the task belongs to research, review, or implementation planning.
runner_instructions.environment: Filesystem write access is optional unless the result is being saved to a repository file as part of the evaluation.
